prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_medication_order refrig_order_1)
(allocate_drug_product_to_order refrig_order_1 drug_product_a)
(select_drug_variant_for_order refrig_order_1 variant_a)
(confirm_medication_selection_for_order refrig_order_1 variant_a)
(reserve_cold_storage_slot_for_order refrig_order_1 cold_slot_1 variant_a)
(assign_staff_verifier_to_order refrig_order_1 staff_member_1)
(staff_confirm_cold_storage_reservation refrig_order_1 cold_slot_1 staff_member_1)
(revert_selected_drug_variant_for_order refrig_order_1 variant_a)
(register_medication_order frozen_order_1)
(allocate_drug_product_to_order frozen_order_1 drug_product_b)
(select_drug_variant_for_order frozen_order_1 variant_a)
(confirm_medication_selection_for_order frozen_order_1 variant_a)
(reserve_fulfillment_slot_for_frozen_order frozen_order_1 fulfillment_slot_1 variant_a)
(unassign_staff_verifier_from_order refrig_order_1 staff_member_1)
(assign_staff_verifier_to_order frozen_order_1 staff_member_1)
(staff_confirm_fulfillment_reservation frozen_order_1 fulfillment_slot_1 staff_member_1)
(revert_selected_drug_variant_for_order frozen_order_1 variant_a)
(select_drug_variant_for_order refrig_order_1 variant_a)
(allocate_container_and_bind_slots_for_batch refrig_order_1 frozen_order_1 cold_slot_1 fulfillment_slot_1 dispensing_container_1)
(mark_container_ready_for_labeling dispensing_container_1 refrig_order_1 variant_a)
(register_medication_order dispense_batch_1)
(allocate_drug_product_to_order dispense_batch_1 drug_product_c)
(revert_selected_drug_variant_for_order refrig_order_1 variant_a)
(select_drug_variant_for_order dispense_batch_1 variant_a)
(confirm_medication_selection_for_order dispense_batch_1 variant_a)
(stage_label_template_to_batch dispense_batch_1 label_template_1 dispensing_container_1)
(assign_packaging_device_to_batch dispense_batch_1 label_template_1 dispensing_container_1 variant_a)
(bind_packaging_device_to_batch dispense_batch_1 packaging_device_1)
(bind_temperature_monitor_to_batch dispense_batch_1 temp_monitor_1)
(unassign_staff_verifier_from_order frozen_order_1 staff_member_1)
(assign_staff_verifier_to_order dispense_batch_1 staff_member_1)
(execute_packaging_operation_variant_a dispense_batch_1 packaging_device_1 staff_member_1 label_template_1 dispensing_container_1)
(finalize_packaging_with_temperature_monitor_variant_a dispense_batch_1 temp_monitor_1 label_template_1 dispensing_container_1)
(finalize_batch_and_mark_release_pending dispense_batch_1)
(apply_release_authorization_to_order dispense_batch_1 release_token_1 variant_a)
(authorize_and_release_batch dispense_batch_1 drug_product_c release_token_1)
(register_medication_order dispense_batch_2)
(allocate_drug_product_to_order dispense_batch_2 drug_product_c)
(revert_selected_drug_variant_for_order dispense_batch_1 variant_a)
(select_drug_variant_for_order dispense_batch_2 variant_a)
(confirm_medication_selection_for_order dispense_batch_2 variant_a)
(stage_label_template_to_batch dispense_batch_2 label_template_2 dispensing_container_1)
(assign_packaging_device_to_batch dispense_batch_2 label_template_2 dispensing_container_1 variant_a)
(unbind_packaging_device_from_batch dispense_batch_1 packaging_device_1)
(bind_packaging_device_to_batch dispense_batch_2 packaging_device_1)
(unbind_temperature_monitor_from_batch dispense_batch_1 temp_monitor_1)
(bind_temperature_monitor_to_batch dispense_batch_2 temp_monitor_1)
(unassign_staff_verifier_from_order dispense_batch_1 staff_member_1)
(assign_staff_verifier_to_order dispense_batch_2 staff_member_1)
(execute_packaging_operation_variant_a dispense_batch_2 packaging_device_1 staff_member_1 label_template_2 dispensing_container_1)
(finalize_packaging_with_temperature_monitor_variant_a dispense_batch_2 temp_monitor_1 label_template_2 dispensing_container_1)
(finalize_batch_and_mark_release_pending dispense_batch_2)
(apply_release_authorization_to_order dispense_batch_2 release_token_1 variant_a)
(authorize_and_release_batch dispense_batch_2 drug_product_c release_token_1)
(mark_refrigerated_order_final_check_complete refrig_order_1 dispensing_container_1)
(mark_frozen_order_final_check_complete frozen_order_1 dispensing_container_1)
(revert_selected_drug_variant_for_order dispense_batch_2 variant_a)
(select_drug_variant_for_order refrig_order_1 variant_a)
(apply_release_authorization_to_order refrig_order_1 release_token_1 variant_a)
(authorize_and_release_refrigerated_order refrig_order_1 drug_product_a release_token_1)
(revert_selected_drug_variant_for_order refrig_order_1 variant_a)
(select_drug_variant_for_order frozen_order_1 variant_a)
(apply_release_authorization_to_order frozen_order_1 release_token_1 variant_a)
(authorize_and_release_frozen_order frozen_order_1 drug_product_b release_token_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for cold storage medicine dispense readiness. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task involves creating a plan for cold storage medicine dispensing readiness using PDDL.  
- The output must be a sequence of ground PDDL actions, each on a new line, forming an executable plan.

## Examining Initial State  
- Objects include refrigerated and frozen medication orders, dispense batches, drugs, staff, slots, containers, labels, safety items, instructions, and devices.  
- Initial facts show products (a, b, c), variant_a, staff_member_1, and substitution_option_1 are available, with specific assignments like refrig_order_1 to cold_slot_1.  

## Identifying Availability Issues  
- Both dispense batches are assigned to the same dispensing_container_1 and labels, with batch1 using label1 and batch2 using label2.  
- Priority_code_1 is explicitly available, but batch2 is assigned priority_code_2, which isn’t listed as available, suggesting a potential oversight in the initial state.

## Analyzing Release Process  
- To make medication orders ready for release, steps like registration, product allocation, and staff assignment are needed, but many are pre-assigned here.  
- No orders are registered yet, and no product allocation is in place, suggesting initial steps are required.

## Checking Order Hierarchy  
- All objects (refrigerated/frozen orders, dispense batches) are subtypes of medication_order, with dispense_batch also being a medication_order.  
- The goal involves both individual orders and batches, with batches containing both refrigerated and frozen orders, creating a complex setup.

## Exploring Authorization Steps  
- To achieve order_ready_for_release, actions like authorize_and_release_order require order_authorized, product allocation, and release authorization.  
- Order authorization needs final verification, variant selection, and an available release token, setting the stage for further steps.  

## Identifying Final Verification Options  
- Final verification can be achieved through actions like finalize_batch_and_mark_release_pending or mark_refrigerated/frozen_order_final_check_complete, depending on the order type.  
- The presence of both batches and individual orders in the goal suggests multiple paths to completion.

## Exploring Batch Processing  
- For normal operations, use allocate_container_and_bind_slots_for_batch to stage refrigerated and frozen orders, like refrig_order_1 and frozen_order_1, with cold_slot_1 and fulfillment_slot_1.  
- This action assigns orders to dispensing_container_1, setting the stage for further labeling and packaging steps.  

## Checking Labeling Requirements  
- Mark_container_ready_for_labeling requires a refrigerated order with selected variant, like refrig_order_1 and variant_a, and uses refrigerated packaging readiness.  
- Batches need medication_selection_confirmed to stage label templates, which isn’t set yet, requiring prior steps like registration and product allocation.  

## Analyzing Product Allocation  
- With only three drug products (a, b, c) for four orders, releasing one order frees its product, allowing re-allocation to another order.  
- This recycling approach enables all four orders to reach order_ready_for_release simultaneously, as long as authorize_and_release actions are sequenced properly.

## Examining Staff and Device Assignments  
- Assigning packaging devices to batches requires staff assignment, medication selection confirmation, and either safety checks or direct allocation depending on container needs.  
- Actions like assign_packaging_device_to_batch and apply_safety_check_and_assign_packaging_device set packaging_device_assigned, with effects varying based on safety check requirements.  

## Checking Labeling Prerequisites  
- Staging labels for batches depends on the dispensing container being ready_for_labeling, achieved by processing contained patient orders first.  
- Patient orders need slots confirmed and containers allocated before batches can proceed, ensuring all prerequisites are met for labeling.  

## Exploring Product and Variant Management  
- With only variant_a available, selection may require reverting if shared across orders, using revert_selected_drug_variant_for_order to make it available again.  
- Registering and allocating products to patient orders, like drug_product_a to refrig_order_1, sets the stage for variant selection and confirmation.

## Extending Variant Management  
- Reverting variant selection for frozen_order_1 makes variant_a available again, allowing reassignment to refrig_order_1.  
- Managing a single variant, like variant_a, requires careful sequencing to avoid conflicts across orders and batches.  

## Processing Batch Registration  
- Registering batches, like dispense_batch_1, involves allocating products (e.g., drug_product_c) and selecting variants, but availability issues may arise.  
- Batches need medication selection confirmed before staging labels, similar to patient orders, but don’t require separate slot reservations.  

## Checking Device Binding Steps  
- Binding packaging devices to batches requires medication selection confirmation and device availability, happening early in the process.  
- Both packaging_device_assigned (from assign_packaging_device_to_batch) and batch_has_packaging_device_bound (from bind_packaging_device_to_batch) are needed for execute packaging operation.  

## Analyzing Labeling Readiness  
- Staging labels for batches needs the dispensing container ready_for_labeling, achieved by processing contained orders first.  
- Batches must have assigned containers, labels, and confirmed selections before proceeding to device assignments and labeling.

## Managing Staff Assignments  
- Unassigning staff_member_1 from frozen_order_1 allows reassigning to dispense_batch_1 for packaging operations.  
- Assigning staff_member_1 to dispense_batch_1 ensures staffing requirements are met for executing packaging tasks.  

## Executing Packaging Steps  
- Executing packaging for dispense_batch_1 requires packaging_device_1 assigned, bound, and staff confirmed, using label_template_1 and dispensing_container_1.  
- Finalizing packaging with temperature_monitor_1 marks dispense_batch_1 as having completed packaging steps, ready for release pending.  

## Authorizing Batch Release  
- Applying release authorization to dispense_batch_1 with release_token_1 and variant_a sets the batch as authorized, consuming the token.  
- Releasing dispense_batch_1 makes drug_product_c available again, completing the batch process.  

## Preparing Batch 2 Operations  
- Registering dispense_batch_2 allows allocating available drug_product_c, previously freed from batch 1.  
- Reverting variant_a from dispense_batch_1 makes it available for selection in dispense_batch_2, enabling further processing.

## Managing Variant Selection  
- Variants must be selected for each order before authorizing release, but only one variant can be selected at a time due to availability constraints.  
- To handle multiple orders, select a variant for one order, authorize and release it, then revert and select for the next order to proceed.  

## Authorizing Refrigerated Orders  
- Applying release authorization to refrig_order_1 with release_token_1 and variant_a requires prior selection, freeing product_a and token upon release.  
- Reverting variant_a after release allows selecting it for frozen_order_1, ensuring sequential processing without conflicts.  

## Handling Frozen Order Release  
- Select variant_a for frozen_order_1 after reverting from refrig_order_1, then apply release authorization with release_token_1.  
- Authorizing and releasing frozen_order_1 frees product_b and token, marking both orders ready for release.  

## Reviewing Unused Resources  
- Priority_code_2 was assigned to dispense_batch_2 but not used, as drug_product_c allocation sufficed after batch1 release.  
- Substitution_option_1 and staff assignments for batches became unnecessary after initial steps, with no impact on process completion.

## Processing Batch Operations  
- Assigning packaging devices and temperature monitors to dispense_batch_1 involves binding packaging_device_1 and temp_monitor_1 for packaging operations.  
- Executing packaging for dispense_batch_1 with variant_a uses staff_member_1, label_template_1, and dispensing_container_1, finalizing with temperature monitoring.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
